user_query:
Curate a mini-anthology of tech‑tinged ghost stories and sci‑fi vignettes for our internal newsletter. Read input/prompts.json for the seed prompts and length/tagline constraints. For each prompt in that file, write a 150–220 word micro‑story in an atmospheric tone using the specified genre (ghost or sci‑fi). Requirements:

1) Write a newline‑delimited JSON file at output/tales.jsonl with one object per story using this schema:
   {"id": "slug-from-title", "title": "Unique Title", "genre": "ghost|sci-fi", "prompt": "<exact prompt text>", "tagline": "<=90 characters", "body": "<story text>", "word_count": <integer>}
   - The body must include the exact prompt sentence verbatim somewhere.
   - Title must be unique across all entries.
   - id must be a lowercase hyphenated slug of the title (letters/digits/hyphens only; spaces replaced by hyphens; other punctuation removed).
   - word_count should reflect the actual number of words in body (150–220 inclusive).

2) Create output/index.md that includes:
   - An H1 heading for the collection.
   - An "Editor's Foreword" section 120–200 words long that references at least two story titles by name.
   - A table of contents listing each story title with its genre tag and tagline on the same line.

3) Create output/qc_report.json summarizing: {"total": <int>, "by_genre": {"ghost": <int>, "sci-fi": <int>}, "avg_word_count": <number>}.

Ensure: (a) total stories equal the number of prompts in input/prompts.json, (b) at least two stories per genre, (c) all JSON is valid. Keep everything in English and write only to the output/ directory.
